summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the total energy in undamped SHM?

Total mechanical energy — E = ½kA² (constant, if no damping). Total energy is a horizontal line on an Energy vs. The other options — 'E = ½mv²', 'E = mgh', and 'E varies continuously' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

At equilibrium (x = 0), what is the energy distribution?

The distributive property: a(b + c) = ab + ac. Multiply each term inside the parentheses by the outside factor. The answer is All kinetic, zero potential. The other options — 'All potential, zero kinetic', 'Equal KE and PE', and 'Zero total energy' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

summary_sections.title: Special Topics
summary_sections.content_html:
Important Concepts

KE and PE oscillate at what frequency compared to the motion

Graphs KE and PE are both sinusoidal and oscillate at twice the frequency of the motion. The other options — 'Same frequency', 'Half the frequency', and 'Three times' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
